headlines.headline: France to develop ballistic missile defense with Ukraine
headlines.summary: President Zelensky announced France's readiness to develop ballistic missile defense capabilities with Ukraine, calling it an "important step" in strengthening capabilities against Russian attacks.

headlines.headline: US waiver for Russian oil purchases expires without renewal
headlines.summary: A US waiver that allowed purchases of Russian oil stranded at sea has expired as of May 16, potentially affecting global energy markets amid ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Putin simplifies Russian citizenship for Transnistria residents
headlines.summary: Moldova's President Maia Sandu warned that Putin's decree simplifying citizenship for Transnistria residents is likely a recruitment tool to send more people to fight in Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Iran to announce Hormuz traffic management mechanism
headlines.summary: Iran is preparing a new mechanism to regulate shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, including designated routes and service fees, with European countries reportedly negotiating passage permissions.

headlines.headline: Japan to bolster sea lane defense with Southeast Asia info-sharing
headlines.summary: Japan announced plans to strengthen sea lane defense through information sharing with Southeast Asian countries, amid growing regional tensions and supply chain concerns.

headlines.headline: BlackRock weighs $5-10 billion investment in SpaceX IPO
headlines.summary: BlackRock has discussed investing $5-10 billion in SpaceX's initial public offering next month, the Information reported, marking one of the largest potential IPO investments by the asset management giant.

headlines.headline: Russia captures two villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv region
headlines.summary: Russian troops have taken control of the settlements of Borova and Kutkivka in Ukraine's Kharkiv region, according to Russia's RIA state news agency citing the Defence Ministry.

headlines.headline: Iran's stock market to reopen Tuesday after war suspension
headlines.summary: Iran will reopen its stock market on Tuesday after a suspension during the conflict with the US and Israel, IRNA news agency cited a senior official as saying, as the country begins normalizing economic activity.

headlines.headline: Canada deepens Arctic defense ties with Nordics amid US uncertainty
headlines.summary: Canada is strengthening defense ties with Nordic countries as Prime Minister Mark Carney builds alliances among "middle powers" in a world where the United States is considered a less reliable partner.

headlines.headline: US charges Iraqi man in Iran-linked terror plots against Jews
headlines.summary: An Iraqi national has been arrested and brought to the US to face terrorism charges for involvement in multiple Iran-backed plots targeting US interests in Europe and Jewish communities.

headlines.headline: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in US-Nigeria Joint Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint US-Nigerian military operation in northeastern Nigeria. President Trump and Nigerian President Tinubu confirmed the operation, marking a significant blow to the terrorist organization.

headlines.headline: Tens of Thousands Protest in London as Rival Rallies Face Off
headlines.summary: London deployed thousands of police officers to manage rival protests: a far-right "Unite the Kingdom" rally and a pro-Palestinian Nakba Day march. At least 11 people were arrested as authorities worked to keep the opposing groups separated.

headlines.headline: IDF Infantry Commander Killed by Hezbollah Drone in Southern Lebanon
headlines.summary: Captain Maoz Israel Recanati, 24, was killed by a Hezbollah drone attack in southern Lebanon. He was set to marry next month. The IDF struck over 100 targets in response.

headlines.headline: Jerusalem Governorate Warns of Israeli Plan to Seize Properties Near Al-Aqsa
headlines.summary: Palestinian authorities warn that Israel plans to approve seizure of Palestinian properties in the Bab al-Silsila neighborhood near Al-Aqsa Mosque on Sunday.

headlines.headline: Trump Not Optimistic on Jimmy Lai Release After Raising Case With Xi
headlines.summary: US President Trump says he raised the case of imprisoned Hong Kong media tycoon Jimmy Lai with Chinese President Xi, but is not optimistic about his release. Lai was sentenced to 20 years under Hong Kong national security law.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah Urges Lebanon to Abandon Peace Illusions With Israel
headlines.summary: Hezbollah warned Lebanese authorities against direct negotiations with Israel as Washington pushes forward with a new round of talks between Beirut and Tel Aviv.

headlines.headline: Putin to visit China days after Trump summit as Russia seeks strategic support
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet Xi Jinping in Beijing next week, just after Trump became the first US president to visit China since 2017.

perspectives.angle: Putin to Visit China Days After Trump Trip
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin will meet Chinese leader Xi Jinping on a two-day trip to Beijing next week. The announcement comes just after Trump wrapped up the first visit to China by a US president in years.

perspectives.angle: Putin to Visit China Soon as Kremlin Announces Completed Preparations
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin will visit Beijing very soon, the Kremlin said, with preparations complete. The visit comes days after Trump-Xi summit, raising speculation about triangular diplomacy between US, China, and Russia.

headlines.headline: Israel kills Hamas military chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza strike
headlines.summary: An Israeli airstrike killed the head of Hamas armed wing, the most senior Hamas official killed since the October ceasefire. Israel says he helped plan the Oct 7 attacks.

perspectives.angle: Hamas military chief dies after Israeli airstrike
perspectives.summary: A senior Hamas official confirmed the death of the groups military wing commander, a day after Israel announced it had carried out airstrikes targeting him. This marks a significant blow to Hamas leadership.

headlines.headline: Romania finds unexploded projectile near Ukraine border
headlines.summary: NATO member Romania discovered an unexploded projectile on property near its Ukraine border, highlighting spillover risks from the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Comoros suspends fuel price hikes after deadly Iran-war protests
headlines.summary: The East African island nation backed down on fuel price increases triggered by the Iran conflict after protests turned deadly across the archipelago.

perspectives.angle: Comoros fuel protests turn deadly amid Iran war price spikes
perspectives.summary: One person killed and five injured in clashes on the Comorian island of Anjouan as unrest over fuel price hikes spreads across the archipelago, linked to global supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz crisis.

headlines.headline: CBS News Live: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Iran Distrusts US
headlines.summary: CBS News provides ongoing coverage of Middle East tensions as Israel conducts strikes in Lebanon while Iran expresses deep mistrust of American involvement in the conflict.

headlines.headline: Iran War Worth Economic Pain, Say Rural Trump Voters
headlines.summary: Despite rising gas prices and economic strain from the Iran conflict, rural Colorado voters remain steadfast in their support for Trump administration policies.

headlines.headline: Diesel Costs From Iran War Drain US School Budgets
headlines.summary: Soaring diesel prices since the onset of the Iran war are straining already tight US school district budgets, making it more expensive to bus students.

headlines.headline: Colombia Mayor Shot Dead During Election Campaign
headlines.summary: A former mayor allied with presidential candidate Abelardo de La Espriella was assassinated in central Colombia amid escalating election violence.

headlines.headline: S&P Upgrades Nigeria on Improving Macroeconomic Profile
headlines.summary: Credit ratings agency S&P upgraded Nigeria long-term sovereign rating to B from B-, citing improving creditworthiness and economic reforms.

headlines.headline: Iran Plans Fees for Ships Passing Through Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran is preparing to charge fees for commercial vessels transiting the strategic Strait of Hormuz amid tensions with the US and Israel. Only vessels from cooperative countries would benefit under the proposed mechanism, tightening control over a critical oil transit route.

headlines.headline: Over 50 Schoolchildren Including Toddlers Kidnapped in Nigeria
headlines.summary: Gunmen abducted more than 50 children, many aged 2-5 years old, from three schools in Mussa, Borno state. Attackers used children as human shields while fleeing on motorbikes. No group has claimed responsibility, though the attack bears hallmarks of Boko Haram activity in the region.

headlines.headline: Israel Threatens Defamation Lawsuit Against NYT Over Sexual Abuse Report
headlines.summary: Israel's Prime Minister Netanyahu and Foreign Minister Sa'ar announced plans to sue the New York Times over a Nicholas Kristof essay alleging sexual abuse of Palestinian prisoners. The Times defended the reporting as rigorously fact-checked, while media law experts questioned whether such a lawsuit by a government could proceed.

headlines.headline: Rwandan genocide suspect Lucien Kabuga dies at 93 in UN custody
headlines.summary: Lucien Kabuga, accused of financing the 1994 genocide in Rwanda, has died in custody at age 93. He was one of the last remaining suspects from the international tribunal.

headlines.headline: BlackRock weighs $5-10B investment in SpaceX IPO
headlines.summary: BlackRock is in discussions to invest billions in SpaceX upcoming initial public offering. The investment would be one of the largest pre-IPO commitments in recent market history.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer set to approve $24B defense spending increase
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound increase in defense spending as he faces political pressure. The Times reports the increase comes amid global security concerns.

headlines.headline: Eight killed as freight train hits bus in Thailand
headlines.summary: At least eight people died and 32 were injured when a freight train struck a bus at a rail crossing in Bangkok, igniting a fire that engulfed the vehicle.

headlines.headline: Sierra Leone agrees to accept hundreds of West Africans deported by US
headlines.summary: Sierra Leone has struck a deal with the Trump administration to accept hundreds of deported West African migrants, the latest in a series of agreements aimed at accelerating US removals.

headlines.headline: France Ready to Develop Ballistic Missile Defense With Ukraine, Zelensky Says
headlines.summary: French President discussed strengthening capabilities to repel Russian attacks, with Zelensky calling the cooperation an important step in air defense development.

perspectives.angle: France to develop ballistic missile defense system with Ukraine
perspectives.summary: President Zelensky announced France's readiness to cooperate on ballistic missile defense, calling it an important step in strengthening Ukraine's ability to repel Russian attacks.

headlines.headline: Russians Worry More About Strikes at Home Than Front Line for First Time Since 2022
headlines.summary: A new poll shows shifting public anxiety inside Russia, with growing concern about domestic attacks versus battlefield risks as Ukrainian drone strikes intensify.

perspectives.angle: Poll: Russians fear strikes at home more than front line for first time since 2022
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian drone strikes deep inside Russia have shifted Russian public concern from battlefield developments to domestic security, according to a new poll.

headlines.headline: US-Israel Preparing for Possible Renewal of Strikes on Iran, Report Says
headlines.summary: Haaretz reports preparations underway for potential new strikes against Iranian targets as diplomatic negotiations remain stalled.

headlines.headline: US CEOs Pursue Diplomacy in Beijing Following Trump Summit
headlines.summary: American business leaders from Citigroup and Goldman Sachs meet Chinese officials, hoping political goodwill will translate into regulatory approvals and lucrative deals.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Asserts Independence After Trump Warning Post-Beijing Summit
perspectives.summary: Taiwan's foreign ministry declared the island is a 'sovereign and independent democratic nation' after President Trump warned against formal independence declarations following his summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing. Trump said he opposes Taiwan going independent and questioned why the US would fight a war 9,500 miles away.

perspectives.angle: Trump-Xi Summit Concludes With Trade Deals, Taiwan Tensions Loom
perspectives.summary: President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping wrapped up their Beijing summit with promises of 'fantastic trade deals' including Chinese purchases of US oil, soybeans, and Boeing jets. However, Xi warned of 'conflicts' if the Taiwan issue is mishandled, while Trump made no commitments on arms sales to Taiwan or political prisoners.

perspectives.angle: New York Times: Xi and Trump Strike Positive Tone After Taiwan Warning
perspectives.summary: Following Xi's stark warning on Taiwan, both leaders emphasized the importance of US-China relations in their summit discussions on trade, Iran war, and bilateral cooperation.

headlines.headline: Russia Captures Two Villages in Ukraine's Kharkiv Region
headlines.summary: Russian forces have taken control of Borova and Kutkivka settlements in Kharkiv, marking continued territorial gains as Moscow presses its offensive in eastern Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Russia captures two villages in Ukraines Kharkiv region
perspectives.summary: Russian forces have taken control of Borova and Kutkivka settlements in Kharkiv, according to Russias Defence Ministry cited by RIA. The advance comes amid continued fighting in eastern Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO from Belarus
headlines.summary: Ukraine's president says Moscow is seeking to draw Belarus deeper into the war and weighing plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory.

perspectives.angle: Zelenskiy Warns Russia May Attack NATO From Belarus
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy stated that Russia is considering plans to attack Ukraine's north or a NATO country from Belarusian territory, drawing Belarus deeper into the war.

headlines.headline: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Nations
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Carney strengthens alliances with "middle powers" as the US becomes a less reliable partner, focusing on Arctic security amid growing geopolitical tensions.

perspectives.angle: Canada Deepens Arctic Defense Ties With Nordic Countries After Trump Greenland Threats
perspectives.summary: Following Trump's threats to seize Greenland, Canadian authorities have been strengthening defense cooperation with Nordic countries. Greenland has been seeking support from its northern ally Canada.

headlines.headline: Consumers Sue Amazon Over Unlawful Trump Tariff Costs
headlines.summary: Amazon faces lawsuit from consumers seeking refunds for higher prices resulting from tariffs the Supreme Court later ruled were unlawfully imposed by President Trump.

headlines.headline: Latvia faces political uncertainty as president names opposition lawmaker as PM candidate
headlines.summary: Latvian President Edgars Rinkevics proposed opposition lawmaker Andris Kulbergs as the next prime minister after Evika Silina resigned, amid regional security concerns.

headlines.headline: US and Brazil ethanol exports surge as countries seek fuel alternatives
headlines.summary: The world's two largest ethanol producers are seeing export increases as consuming countries seek alternative fuel sources while the Strait of Hormuz crisis continues.

headlines.headline: China criticizes US Hormuz resolution at UN as timing and content questioned
headlines.summary: China's UN ambassador criticized a proposed US-Bahraini resolution on the Strait of Hormuz, saying the content and timing were inappropriate and passing it would not be helpful.

headlines.headline: Italy's Meloni wins European backing for third-country migrant return hubs
headlines.summary: Italian PM Giorgia Meloni hailed a shift in European migration policy as her controversial approach to offshore migrant processing gains support across the Council of Europe.

headlines.headline: UAE States All Actions Were Defensive Measures to Protect Sovereignty
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates foreign ministry issued a statement saying all actions taken by the Gulf country were defensive measures aimed at protecting its sovereignty, civilians, and vital infrastructure amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: FIFA Officials to Meet Iranian FA Over World Cup Participation
headlines.summary: FIFA Secretary-General Mattias Grafstrom will meet Iranian Football Federation officials in Istanbul to offer reassurance over Iran participation in the upcoming World Cup amid the ongoing Iran-US conflict.

headlines.headline: Eurovision Song Contest Final Proceeds Amid Gaza Boycott
headlines.summary: Five countries have withdrawn from the Eurovision Song Contest final over Israel participation, with the event taking place against the backdrop of ongoing Middle East tensions.

perspectives.angle: Spain PM backs Eurovision boycott, says silence on Gaza "not an option"
perspectives.summary: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez defended Spain's boycott of Eurovision over Israel's participation, saying it puts the country "on the right side of history" and warning against "double standards" compared to Russia's exclusion.

headlines.headline: US Reportedly Preparing to Indict Former Cuban Leader Raul Castro
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department is reportedly preparing to criminally indict former Cuban leader Raul Castro in the coming days, marking a significant development in US-Cuba relations.

headlines.headline: Congo Confirms New Ebola Outbreak with 80 Deaths
headlines.summary: Africa CDC confirms Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo's Ituri province. Health officials are mobilizing response efforts to contain the spread.

perspectives.angle: Ebola outbreak in Congo reports 80 deaths amid constant burials
perspectives.summary: A new Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo has killed at least 80 people, with locals describing continuous burials as authorities struggle to contain the spread.

headlines.headline: US Proposes Dramatic Colorado River Water Cuts Amid Drought
headlines.summary: US government plans could cut up to 40% of water supplies to Arizona, California and Nevada. The drought-stricken Colorado River basin faces unprecedented pressure from climate change and overuse.

headlines.headline: Trump-Targeted Senator Cassidy Faces Louisiana Primary Test
headlines.summary: Republican Senator Bill Cassidy, targeted by Trump for retribution over impeachment vote, faces competitive primary in Louisiana. Results will test Trump's influence on GOP congressional races.

headlines.headline: OpenAI Signs Deal to Provide ChatGPT Plus to All Maltese Citizens
headlines.summary: OpenAI partners with Malta's government to give all residents access to ChatGPT Plus for one year following AI training course. First nationwide AI access program of its kind.

headlines.headline: Israel and Lebanon Extend Ceasefire by 45 Days
headlines.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by 45 days to enable further diplomatic progress, the U.S. State Department announced, even as Israel continues strikes it says fall outside the truce agreement.

perspectives.angle: Israel Strikes Lebanon Hours After Ceasefire Extension
perspectives.summary: Israel launched new airstrikes against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon just hours after the US announced a 45-day ceasefire extension. The April 17 truce did not end Israeli strikes in Lebanese territory, and Iran expressed distrust of US negotiations.

perspectives.angle: Israeli strikes on southern Lebanon continue despite ceasefire extension
perspectives.summary: Despite Israel and Lebanon agreeing to extend their ceasefire for 45 more days, Israeli warplanes continued striking villages in southern Lebanon. Hezbollah opposes the negotiations as Israeli forces maintain strikes and occupation in parts of the south.

headlines.headline: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
headlines.summary: After a year of concessions on tariffs and conflicts, U.S. President Donald Trump's confrontational negotiating style has met its match in Iran, where neither threats nor military action have produced the desired outcome.

perspectives.angle: Analysis: Trump's Geopolitical Brinkmanship Hits Wall With Iran
perspectives.summary: Reuters analysis examines how President Trump's blustery negotiating style, which won concessions on other issues, has faced significant challenges with Iran amid ongoing tensions over nuclear weapons and the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Trump Returns From China With Stability and Stalemate
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump's Beijing summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping produced modest results but highlighted China's desire for stability after last year's trade war extremes, with the US-China relationship now defined by managed competition.

headlines.headline: China Confirms Boeing Aircraft Purchase Deal Following Trump Visit
headlines.summary: Chinese Ministry of Commerce confirms Beijing will buy at least 200 Boeing aircraft and engines, marking a significant trade concession following Trump's summit with Xi.

perspectives.angle: China Doubles Down on Manufacturing Push After Trump Visit
perspectives.summary: China's Communist Party journal calls for strengthening manufacturing shortly after Trump's departure from Beijing, signaling continued focus on industrial self-sufficiency amid trade tensions.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Talks With Iranian Officials
headlines.summary: Pakistan's Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi visits Tehran amid regional tensions, as Pakistan navigates relations with Iran during the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Moscow Signals Frustration as US-Russia Contacts Remain Difficult
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Ryabkov indicates that US-Russia diplomatic contacts remain slow and difficult amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: ISIS Deputy Leader Killed in Joint US-Nigeria Operation
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, the second-in-command of ISIS globally, was killed in a joint operation by US and Nigerian forces in northeastern Nigeria. Both presidents Trump and Tinubu confirmed the elimination of the terrorist leader.

headlines.headline: US Accuses Iraqi Man of Helping Iran-Backed Militia Plot Attacks in US and Europe
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department announced the arrest of an Iraqi national accused of involvement in multiple attacks against US interests in Europe and planning attacks on US soil for an Iran-backed militia.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Presses US for Arms Sales After Trump Undecided on Future Deliveries
headlines.summary: Taiwan's government emphasized that US arms sales are essential for regional peace and deterrence, following President Trump's statement that he has not decided on future weapons sales to the island.

headlines.headline: Council of Europe Approves Special Tribunal to Try Russian Leadership for Aggression
headlines.summary: The Council of Europe has approved steps to establish a Nuremberg-style tribunal to prosecute Russia's leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine. 36 countries and the EU have committed to supporting the Special Tribunal.

headlines.headline: GOP Rep. Massie Introduces AIPAC Act Targeting Foreign Lobbying Days Before Primary
headlines.summary: Rep. Thomas Massie, the harshest Republican critic of Israel in Congress, introduced legislation aimed at foreign lobbying efforts just days before his Kentucky primary. The bill targets AIPAC and similar organizations.

headlines.headline: Turkish Prosecutors Expand Istanbul Corruption Investigation Targeting Opposition Municipality
headlines.summary: Turkish police detained 12 suspects as part of an expanding corruption investigation into alleged irregularities in municipal tenders linked to Istanbul's opposition-run metropolitan municipality. The probe has intensified political tensions.

headlines.headline: Senior Islamic State leader killed in US-Nigeria joint operation
headlines.summary: US and Nigerian forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described by President Trump as ISIS's second-in-command globally, in a precision strike on his compound in the Lake Chad Basin. Nigerian President Tinubu called it the most consequential counterterrorism outcome since regional operations began in 2015.

perspectives.angle: Nigeria Confirms Killing of Senior ISIS Leader in Joint US Operation
perspectives.summary: Nigerias President Tinubu and military confirmed the killing of ISIS second-in-command Abu-Bilal al-Minuki in Nigeria during a joint US-Nigeria operation.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister to approve 24 billion dollar defense spending increase
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer is expected to approve an 18 billion pound defense spending boost as he faces political challenges. The increase comes amid broader European security concerns and NATO commitments.

headlines.headline: UK Markets Slide on Political Turmoil and Oil Price Fears
headlines.summary: British equities and bonds fell as investors weighed a potential leadership challenge to PM Keir Starmer alongside mounting concerns about oil-driven inflation from the Iran-Hormuz crisis.

headlines.headline: Trump Warns Taiwan: No Blank Check from US Military
headlines.summary: President Trump said Taiwan should not expect a blank check from the US military and stated he is not looking to have someone go independent, in comments about the Taiwan question.

headlines.headline: Trump Says Xi Agrees Iran Must Open Hormuz Strait, China Says War Should Not Have Started
headlines.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Chinese President Xi Jinping agreed Tehran must reopen the Strait of Hormuz after their Beijing summit, though China gave no indication it would intervene militarily in the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US military says 78 ships redirected from Strait of Hormuz amid Iran blockade
perspectives.summary: CENTCOM reports that 78 commercial vessels have been redirected and 4 disabled to ensure compliance with the Hormuz closure, underscoring the escalating disruption to global shipping.

headlines.headline: China Renews Export Licenses for 400+ US Beef Plants After Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: China renewed export licenses that had expired for more than 400 U.S. beef plants, the Chinese customs website showed, a potential sign of thawing trade relations following the Beijing summit between Presidents Trump and Xi.

perspectives.angle: Trump-China Summit Ends With Conciliatory Tone, Few Specifics
perspectives.summary: President Trump concluded his Beijing summit with Xi Jinping, touting "fantastic trade deals" while shifting away from adversarial rhetoric. Details remain scarce as both sides claim progress in stabilizing relations.

perspectives.angle: Trump Leaves China With Warm Rhetoric But Few Concrete Results
perspectives.summary: Trump departed Beijing after a summit rich in pageantry but offering little tangible progress on Iran, Taiwan, or AI. Xi warned of potential conflict over Taiwan while Trump remained noncommittal on arms sales. No major breakthroughs emerged on the key issues dividing the two superpowers.

perspectives.angle: Trump-Xi Summit Ends With Trade Deals But No Major Breakthroughs
perspectives.summary: President Trump concluded his Beijing summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping, touting "fantastic trade deals" including Chinese purchases of U.S. oil, but experts say the agreements lack substance and major issues like Taiwan and Iran remain unresolved.

headlines.headline: Iraq Oil Exports Through Hormuz Drop 90% as Strait Blockade Bites
headlines.summary: Iraq exported just 10 million barrels of oil through the Strait of Hormuz in April, down from about 93 million barrels monthly before the Iran conflict began, the country's new oil minister said, highlighting the severe impact on global energy flows.

perspectives.angle: UAE Accelerates Pipeline to Double Oil Exports Bypassing Hormuz
perspectives.summary: The UAE announced it will accelerate construction of a new oil pipeline to double export capacity through Fujairah by 2027, significantly expanding its ability to bypass the Strait of Hormuz amid Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: US Treasury Yields Hit One-Year High as Oil Prices and Inflation Rattle Markets
headlines.summary: U.S. Treasury yields climbed to their highest levels in a year as a spike in oil prices tied to Middle East energy disruptions, inflation concerns and expectations for a stronger economy led investors to reevaluate interest rate trajectories.

headlines.headline: Chinese Officials Meet Citigroup and Goldman Sachs CEOs in Beijing
headlines.summary: China's securities regulator chairman and Beijing's party secretary held talks with Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser, discussing enhanced cooperation in wealth management and cross-border financing following the Trump-Xi summit.

headlines.headline: Sarmat ICBM Has No Analogues, Can Penetrate Any Defense System
headlines.summary: Russian Deputy Foreign Minister Sergey Ryabkov stated that the Sarmat missile system can penetrate both existing and any missile defense systems still in development.

headlines.headline: Kurdish Groups Reject Syrias Allocation of Only 4 Parliament Seats
headlines.summary: Syrian Kurdish groups rejected the allocation of only four seats for Kurds in Syrias 210-member parliament, calling the representation inadequate.

headlines.headline: Iran FM Says Trust Is Main Obstacle in Nuclear Talks With US
headlines.summary: Iran's foreign minister Abbas Araghchi said a lack of trust is the biggest obstacle in negotiations to end the war with the US, expressing openness to diplomatic help from China. He acknowledged that enriched uranium discussions have hit a deadlock.

perspectives.angle: Iran FM says trust deficit main obstacle in US negotiations
perspectives.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Tehran cannot trust the US and would only negotiate if Washington shows seriousness, during a press conference in New Delhi following a BRICS meeting.

perspectives.angle: Iran doubts US seriousness about nuclear talks amid deadlock
perspectives.summary: Iran Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi expressed distrust of US intentions regarding war negotiations, saying enriched uranium remains a point of deadlock in any potential talks.

headlines.headline: Spain Boycotts Eurovision Over Israel Participation
headlines.summary: Spain's public broadcaster withdrew from the Eurovision Song Contest, protesting Israel's participation over its war against Hamas in Gaza. The boycott marks the first time in seven years Spain will not participate.

headlines.headline: Trump Accepts 20-Year Iran Nuclear Freeze as Sufficient for Deal
headlines.summary: President Trump indicated he would accept a 20-year suspension of Iran nuclear program, a shift from previous demands for permanent cessation. The comments came after his summit with Xi in Beijing, with both sides still far apart on key issues.

headlines.headline: US and Nigerian Forces Kill Senior ISIS Commander
headlines.summary: President Trump announced that US and Nigerian military forces killed Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described as the second-in-command of ISIS globally and "the most active terrorist in the world," in a joint operation in Nigeria.

headlines.headline: Boeing Secures 200-Plane Order From China After Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: Boeing confirmed its first major sale to China in nearly a decade with an order for 200 aircraft. Trump said China reserved the right to purchase up to 750 planes, marking a breakthrough for the aerospace giant.

headlines.headline: Kazakhstan and Turkey Deepen Strategic Ties on Middle Corridor Route
headlines.summary: Presidents Tokayev and Erdogan signed a Declaration on Eternal Friendship and Expanded Strategic Partnership, pledging to raise bilateral trade to $15 billion and expand the Trans-Caspian International Transport Route as a key alternative trade corridor amid regional instability.

headlines.headline: Chinese Defense Firms Claim New Sniper Rifle Range Record
headlines.summary: Two Chinese defense companies announced they may have broken a long-distance shooting record, following last year confirmation of hitting five targets at 3,017 meters with the domestically developed CS/LR24 rifle using 8.6mm rounds.

headlines.headline: Russia and Ukraine exchange 205 prisoners each in latest swap
headlines.summary: The prisoner exchange marks a continued diplomatic effort despite ongoing hostilities, with both sides bringing home service personnel from Russian and Ukrainian captivity.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine-Russia Complete Major POW Exchange, 205 Ukrainians Return Home
perspectives.summary: President Zelensky announced 205 Ukrainian prisoners of war returned from Russian captivity in the first phase of a larger "1,000 for 1,000" swap agreement. Most returning soldiers had been held for nearly four years since the full-scale invasion began.

headlines.headline: Chinese Investor Acquires 120-Year-Old German Textile Machinery Maker
headlines.summary: Family-owned Mayer & Cie, a German circular knitting machine manufacturer with over 80,000 machines worldwide serving brands like H&M and Uniqlo, has been acquired by Chinese investor Huixing amid industry pressure from Chinese competitors.

headlines.headline: Drone warfare becomes deadliest threat to Sudanese civilians
headlines.summary: Armed drones account for over 80% of conflict-related civilian deaths in Sudan, with at least 880 civilians killed by drone strikes between January and April 2026.

headlines.headline: Taiwan presses US for arms sales after Trump signals uncertainty
headlines.summary: Following Trump's comments during his China visit expressing hesitation about defending Taiwan, the island's government emphasizes the importance of US arms deals for regional security.

perspectives.angle: Trump Weighs Taiwan Arms Package After Summit Aimed at Steadying US-China Ties
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump said he has not decided whether to proceed with a major arms sale to Taiwan, adding to uncertainty about US support for the island following his summit with Xi Jinping in Beijing.

headlines.headline: US and Nigerian forces kill ISIS second-in-command, Trump announces
headlines.summary: Abu-Bilal al-Minuki, described as the global second-in-command of ISIS, was eliminated in a joint US-Nigerian military operation, according to President Trump.

headlines.headline: 36 Countries and EU Approve Special Tribunal to Prosecute Putin for Aggression
headlines.summary: A historic legal agreement creates a tribunal to prosecute Russia's top leadership for the crime of aggression against Ukraine, closing a legal loophole that the ICC could not address. Zelensky called it a shield for the future while standing at the site of a Kyiv residential strike that killed 24.

headlines.headline: Japan Considers Missile Exports to Philippines in Historic Defense Shift
headlines.summary: Tokyo last month unveiled its biggest overhaul of defense export rules in decades, scrapping restrictions on overseas arms transfers. Japan may now export missiles to the Philippines, marking a significant shift from its pacifist post-war defense posture.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Civil War Compounded by Distant Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: Farmers in Myanmar face fuel and fertilizer shortages as ships are diverted away from the Strait of Hormuz, hitting agricultural supply chains. The Iran conflict 2,000 miles away is exacerbating hardships in a country already ravaged by civil war since the 2021 coup.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Vows Retribution After Russian Missile Strike Kills 24 in Kyiv
headlines.summary: President Zelenskiy promised retribution after a Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment building killed 24 people including three children, vowing justice for the victims.

perspectives.angle: Zelenskyy Vows Retribution After Russian Strikes Kill 24 in Kyiv
perspectives.summary: President Zelenskyy promised retaliation after a Russian missile strike killed 24 people including three children at a Kyiv apartment building. Ukraine launched retaliatory drone strikes on Russian oil infrastructure as massive Russian bombardments continued following the expiration of a US-brokered ceasefire.

headlines.headline: BRICS Fails to Issue Joint Statement Over Iran War Divisions
headlines.summary: Top diplomats from BRICS nations failed to issue a joint statement after a meeting in Delhi, exposing divisions over the Iran conflict. Host India released only a chair statement.

perspectives.angle: BRICS Foreign Ministers Fail to Agree on Joint Statement Over Iran War
perspectives.summary: Top diplomats from BRICS nations including rivals Iran and UAE could not issue a joint statement after a two-day meeting in Delhi, exposing deep divisions within the bloc over the ongoing Iran conflict. India released only a chair's statement instead.

headlines.headline: Explosions Reported at Russian Chemical Facility in Stavropol Krai
headlines.summary: Overnight explosions and fires were reported in Russias Stavropol and Tatarstan regions, according to local media outlets, adding to a series of Ukrainian drone attacks on Russian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Latvia government collapses over Ukrainian drone controversy
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after her coalition fell apart following disputes over handling stray Ukrainian drones that crossed into Latvian airspace from Russia, raising questions about NATO border defense readiness.

headlines.headline: Putin declares Ukraine conflict coming to an end after scaled-back Victory Day
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin suggested the war in Ukraine could be ending, speaking after a muted Victory Day parade that lacked military hardware due to fears of Ukrainian attacks. He said he would only meet Zelenskyy once a lasting peace deal is agreed.

headlines.headline: Europe faces defense industry hurdles despite new political will on spending
headlines.summary: European NATO allies are finding that boosting defense spending is easier said than done. Decades of reliance on US equipment and fragmented national defense industries pose major obstacles to achieving military self-sufficiency.

headlines.headline: Gulf States Edge Closer to Direct Confrontation with Iran
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia and UAE have conducted military strikes inside Iran in retaliation for Iranian attacks, marking a shift from proxy conflicts to direct engagement. Kuwait also arrested IRGC members plotting attacks on Bubiyan Island.

headlines.headline: Pentagon Halts Troop Deployments to Poland and Germany
headlines.summary: The Pentagon is drawing down thousands of troops in Europe by canceling planned deployments to Poland and Germany. The move has drawn bipartisan criticism from US lawmakers concerned about signaling weakness to Russia.

perspectives.angle: US Halts Poland Troop Deployment After Germany Withdrawal Order
perspectives.summary: The Pentagon has stopped the deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland following Trump administration orders to pull thousands of soldiers from Germany. The move comes amid tensions over Chancellor Merz comments on US handling of Iran.

headlines.headline: Putin Signs Decree Simplifying Russian Citizenship for Transnistria Residents
headlines.summary: Eligible applicants in the breakaway Moldovan region can skip Russian residency requirements, history and civic tests, and need not prove proficiency in the Russian language, signaling Moscow continued influence efforts in the region.

headlines.headline: US Army Creates Multi-Domain Command-Pacific to Counter China Threats
headlines.summary: The US Army is merging the 7th Infantry Division and 1st Multi-Domain Task Force into a new two-star command for the Indo-Pacific. The command will integrate long-range strike, cyber, electronic warfare, and intelligence capabilities across the region.

headlines.headline: Xi Jinping to Make First US State Visit in Over a Decade This Autumn
headlines.summary: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi confirmed Xi Jinping will visit the US at Trump's invitation this autumn. Trump said the leaders could meet three more times this year, including at APEC in Shenzhen and G20 in Miami.

headlines.headline: Pentagon draws down thousands of troops in Europe
headlines.summary: The Pentagon is reducing US troop levels in Europe by canceling planned deployments to Poland and Germany rather than withdrawing existing forces, according to AP sources. The move comes as the Trump administration reassesses America's military footprint abroad.

headlines.headline: Oil prices surge over 3% as US-Iran standoff persists
headlines.summary: Oil prices jumped more than 3% as comments from President Trump and Iran's foreign minister dented hopes for a deal to end ship attacks and seizures around the Strait of Hormuz. UAE stocks also fell as the deadlock rattled regional markets.

perspectives.angle: Oil prices rise as Trump signals impatience with Iran over Hormuz attacks
perspectives.summary: Crude oil gained over 1% as President Trump said he would not be much more patient with Iran, with concerns persisting over ship attacks and seizures despite Tehran claiming 30 vessels had passed through the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.headline: Venezuela launches massive sovereign debt restructuring
headlines.summary: Venezuela began a comprehensive public debt restructuring, one of the largest and most complex sovereign reworks on record. The country still faces major hurdles to complete the process after years of economic crisis and sanctions.

headlines.headline: Merz: Would Advise Children Against Living in US
headlines.summary: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he would advise his children against living or studying in the United States, citing a rapidly changing social climate and limited opportunities even for the highly educated.

perspectives.angle: German Chancellor Merz Would Not Advise Children to Go to US
perspectives.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz said he would not advise his children to study or work in the United States, no longer viewing the country as a land of opportunity amid its polarized political climate.

headlines.headline: Greek Tanker Crosses Hormuz Amid Gulf Shipping Disruption
headlines.summary: A Greek-operated tanker sailed from the Gulf to India, one of the few crude sailings through the Strait of Hormuz this week as most shipping remains disrupted by the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: India-UAE Sign Strategic Defense Partnership
headlines.summary: India and the UAE agreed on a strategic defence partnership framework as PM Modi visited Abu Dhabi, deepening ties amid the Iran war with agreements on defence and petroleum cooperation.

headlines.headline: US Arrests Iraqi Kataib Hezbollah Commander for Plotting Attacks on Jewish Targets
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department arrested Mohammad Baqer Saad Dawood Al-Saadi, an Iraqi national and Kataib Hezbollah commander, for terrorism on behalf of Iran. He allegedly planned attacks against Jewish sites and US interests.

headlines.headline: UAE Unveils $5 Billion India Investment Push During Modi Visit
headlines.summary: The UAE signed deals with India related to energy supplies and maritime security during Prime Minister Modi's visit, strengthening ties between the two nations amid the regional conflict.

headlines.headline: IMF Warns Hong Kong Economy of Risks Linked to Middle East War
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund praised Hong Kong's resilient economy but warned of risks connected to the Middle East conflict while urging the city to pursue medium-term financial reforms.

headlines.headline: Pakistan Secures Release of 31 Seafarers Held by US
headlines.summary: Pakistan announced the successful repatriation of 31 seafarers, including 11 Pakistanis and 20 Iranians, who were aboard vessels seized by the US in a high-seas operation.

headlines.headline: Oscar-Winning Iranian Director Condemns War Deaths and Crackdown
headlines.summary: Asghar Farhadi, director of the first Iranian film to win an Oscar for best foreign language movie, described as deeply painful the deaths of thousands in a January crackdown on protesters and the ongoing Iran war.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department Prepares to Indict Former Cuban President Raul Castro
headlines.summary: The US Justice Department is working to secure criminal charges against former Cuban President Raul Castro related to the 1996 shootdown of humanitarian planes, amid heightened US-Cuba tensions.

headlines.headline: Japan Faces Critical Shortage of Accredited Language Schools for Foreign Workers
headlines.summary: Only three schools nationwide are certified to teach under Japan's new foreign worker training program, creating a bottleneck as the country seeks to address labor shortages through immigration.

headlines.headline: US Army Trains to Counter Drone Swarms as Battlefield Threat Evolves
headlines.summary: The US Army is developing tactics to combat multiple drone threats simultaneously, moving beyond individual counter-drone measures as adversaries increasingly deploy swarm tactics.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine races to deploy AI-powered drone swarms to counter Russian manpower advantage
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian defense industry is advancing swarm drone technology that would allow few operators to deploy dozens or hundreds of autonomous attack craft, though mass battlefield deployment remains years away.

headlines.headline: FBI Offers $200K Reward for Ex-Air Force Specialist Who Defected to Iran
headlines.summary: Monica Witt, a former US Air Force counterintelligence specialist, is wanted for espionage after allegedly defecting to Iran and sharing classified information about US intelligence personnel.

headlines.headline: UN Vehicle Struck Twice by Drones in Ukraine's Kherson Region
headlines.summary: The UN expressed alarm after a humanitarian vehicle was hit twice by drones in Kherson, calling it a serious incident. The organization is working to establish the facts and has not attributed the attack to either side.

headlines.headline: China Agrees to Buy 200 Boeing Jets, Could Rise to 750
headlines.summary: President Trump announced China will purchase 200 Boeing aircraft with GE engines, with potential to expand to 750 jets. The deal emerged from the Beijing summit between Trump and Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: German Chancellor Merz Reports Good Call With Trump After Iran Spat
headlines.summary: Friedrich Merz initiated a phone call with Trump following tensions over Iran policy. Merz, who previously expressed admiration for America, sought to smooth relations after criticizing US actions.

headlines.headline: US Expects Double-Digit Billions in Chinese Farm Purchases After Summit
headlines.summary: USTR Jamieson Greer announced China will commit to massive agricultural purchases following the Trump-Xi summit. The deal aims to reduce trade imbalance between the two nations.

perspectives.angle: US Expects Massive Chinese Farm Purchases After Trump-Xi Summit
perspectives.summary: The US anticipates China will commit to buy double-digit billions worth of American agricultural goods following the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer announced the expected deal as Trump concluded his two-day visit.

headlines.headline: China Still Slow on Rare Earth Approvals Despite Improvements
headlines.summary: USTR Greer noted that while rare earth exports to the US are improving, Beijing continues to delay some shipments. Rare earths remain a key point of leverage in US-China trade negotiations.

headlines.headline: Trump Leaves Beijing With Few Wins But Warm Words for Xi
headlines.summary: President Trump departed China after two days of talks without major breakthroughs on trade or Iran, but with praise for Xi Jinping. Key issues like Taiwan and AI remain unresolved.

perspectives.angle: Trump Leaves China With Few Wins Despite Warm Words for Xi
perspectives.summary: President Trump departed Beijing with no major breakthroughs on trade or concrete Chinese help to end the Iran war, despite two days of praising President Xi. The summit emphasized stability but produced limited tangible results.

headlines.headline: Russian drone force passes 100,000 personnel, copying Ukraine model
headlines.summary: Russia has expanded its drone forces from 86,000 to over 114,000 personnel and is on track to reach 168,000 by year-end, effectively copying Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces structure, according to Ukrainian commander Robert "Magyar" Brovdi.

headlines.headline: UAE rejects Iranian accusations of war involvement
headlines.summary: The UAE Foreign Ministry rejected Iranian claims that Abu Dhabi was an "active partner" in the US-Israel war, calling them attempts to justify Iranian attacks on the Gulf nation. Iran had accused the UAE of involvement after the BRICS meeting in New Delhi.

headlines.headline: Trump Discussed Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
headlines.summary: President Trump revealed he discussed potentially lifting sanctions on Chinese companies purchasing Iranian oil during his Beijing visit, with a decision expected soon.

perspectives.angle: Trump Discusses Lifting Sanctions on Chinese Companies Buying Iranian Oil
perspectives.summary: President Trump said he discussed lifting sanctions on Chinese companies that purchase Iranian oil with Xi Jinping and will make a decision in the coming days, signaling potential policy shift.

headlines.headline: CIA Director Ratcliffe Meets Cuban Officials in Havana
headlines.summary: CIA Director John Ratcliffe held high-level talks with Cuban officials including Raúl Castros grandson, discussing intelligence cooperation and security issues amid tensions over US energy blockade.

headlines.headline: India and UAE sign defense and energy deals during Modi state visit
headlines.summary: India and the UAE signed a framework for a strategic defense partnership and petroleum supply agreements during PM Modi's visit, with the UAE announcing  billion in investments. The deals deepen ties as both nations navigate the Iran conflict's impact on energy markets.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone pilots demonstrate edge during NATO drills on Gotland
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drone pilots showcased advanced tactics during NATO exercises on Sweden's Gotland island, demonstrating lessons from combat experience. Meanwhile, a US House discharge petition forced a vote on nearly  billion in new Ukraine aid.

headlines.headline: Iran war deepens Lebanon's economic crisis with job losses and inflation
headlines.summary: The Iran-Israel conflict has worsened Lebanon's economic crisis, with businesses closing, prices spiking, and job losses mounting. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has sent economic shockwaves across the Middle East.

headlines.headline: Samsung faces 18-day strike as AI boom sparks worker divisions
headlines.summary: South Korean chip giant Samsung faces an impending 18-day strike that has rattled investors and threatens global supply chains, driven by disputes over how AI boom profits should be shared with workers.

headlines.headline: US Cancels Planned Deployment of 4,000 Troops to Poland
headlines.summary: The Pentagon scrapped plans to temporarily deploy 4,000 US-based troops to Poland, raising fresh questions about Trumps expected troop cuts in Europe and NATOs eastern flank security.

perspectives.angle: US cancels planned deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland
perspectives.summary: The Pentagon abruptly cancelled a planned deployment of 4,000 U.S. troops to Poland, with no explanation from Defense Secretary Hegseth. The move follows U.S. plans to withdraw 5,000 troops from Germany, raising concerns about U.S. military commitments to European allies.

headlines.headline: Germany Arrests Ukrainian National Suspected of Spying for Russia
headlines.summary: German prosecutors announced the arrest of a Ukrainian national on suspicion of espionage for Russia. The case highlights ongoing intelligence threats in Europe amid the Ukraine war.

headlines.headline: US Admiral: Iran Military Capability Dramatically Degraded
headlines.summary: The US admiral commanding the Iran war effort said American strikes have left Tehran with only small or moderate ability to strike its neighbors, describing major damage to Irans defense industry.

perspectives.angle: Iran Military Capability Dramatically Degraded, US Admiral Says
perspectives.summary: US Central Command commander said American strikes have dealt a major blow to Iran's military and defense industry, leaving Tehran with only a small or moderate ability to strike its neighbors.

headlines.headline: Iran FM: No Trust in US, Will Negotiate Only If Serious
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi said Tehran has no trust in Washington and will only negotiate if the US shows seriousness. He added that all vessels can pass through Hormuz except those at war with Iran.

headlines.headline: Iran says Pakistan mediation with US at impasse over uranium enrichment
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi stated that Pakistan-mediated talks with the US face deadlock over the enriched uranium issue, while welcoming potential Chinese diplomatic involvement.

headlines.headline: UN agencies warn Somalia faces renewed famine risk as hunger crisis deepens
headlines.summary: Nearly 6 million people face acute food insecurity in Somalia with famine risk emerging in the southwest, as UN agencies call for urgent international assistance.

headlines.headline: Japan Economy Expected to Grow for Second Quarter Despite Iran War Shadow
headlines.summary: Japan's GDP likely rose in January-March on recovering exports and solid domestic demand, a Reuters poll showed, though the full impact of the Iran conflict on the global economy has yet to be felt.

headlines.headline: Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halts Fuel Output After Drone Attack Fire
headlines.summary: Gazprom's gas processing plant in Russia's southern Astrakhan region has halted motor fuel production after a fire broke out following a drone attack on May 13, two industry sources told Reuters.

perspectives.angle: Gazprom halts fuel output at Russian plant after drone strike
perspectives.summary: Gazproms Astrakhan gas processing plant stopped motor fuel production following a fire from a May 13 drone attack, highlighting the reach of aerial warfare into Russian energy infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Iraqi Parliament Approves Partial Government Under New PM Zaidi
headlines.summary: Iraq's new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi was sworn in with only a partial cabinet after lawmakers failed to reach consensus on key postings including interior and defense, amid political uncertainty in a country heavily affected by the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Bolivian Miners Clash With Police in La Paz, Demand President Resign
headlines.summary: Violent clashes erupted in La Paz as mining groups took to the streets calling for the resignation of centrist President Rodrigo Paz, with explosions heard during the protests in Bolivia's capital.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Doubles Strikes on Russian Oil Refineries
headlines.summary: Ukraine has stepped up drone attacks on Russian energy infrastructure, doubling the number of oil refineries targeted since the start of the year, according to Russian officials on social media.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Strikes Russian Aircraft Deep Behind Enemy Lines
perspectives.summary: Zelensky releases footage of Ukrainian strikes on Russian military targets including a Be-200 amphibious aircraft, demonstrating continued long-range strike capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Rescue Teams Pull Bodies From Kyiv Flats After Massive Russian Strikes
perspectives.summary: At least seven people including a girl were killed in Kyiv as Russia launched its largest drone and missile attacks on Ukraine, with rescuers searching through rubble for survivors amid intense bombardment.

headlines.headline: Trump Reaffirms Ironclad US-Japan Alliance After China Summit
headlines.summary: Japanese PM Takaichi confirmed that Trump reaffirmed the ironclad bilateral alliance in a phone call after his Beijing visit, signaling continued US commitment to Asian allies despite Trump-Xi talks.

perspectives.angle: Japan fails to secure Trump stop in Tokyo before Beijing summit
perspectives.summary: Japanese government plans to invite President Trump to Japan ahead of his China visit fell through. The diplomatic setback comes as Trump arrives in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: India Trade Deficit Widens as Mideast War Drives Oil Imports
headlines.summary: India merchandise trade deficit expanded to $28.38 billion in April as crude imports surged to a six-month high, with Middle East conflict disrupting supplies and raising oil prices.

headlines.headline: Israel Adopts Rubble Doctrine in Southern Lebanon Security Policy
headlines.summary: A profound shift in Israeli military thinking after October 7 has led to a new security doctrine focused on denying enemy positions in southern Lebanon through systematic destruction, as seen in El-Khiam.

headlines.headline: Australia Rules Out LNG Export Controls as Gas Supply Fears Ease
headlines.summary: Australia will not impose LNG export controls for Q3 2026 after industry assurances eased concerns about east coast gas supply, maintaining its position as a key global LNG supplier.

headlines.headline: Boeing Shares Drop After China Orders Just 200 Jets
headlines.summary: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing aircraft, far fewer than analysts expected, sending Boeing shares down 4%. The announcement came during Trump’s Beijing visit and disappointed markets hoping for a larger deal.

perspectives.angle: Boeing shares tumble as China agrees to buy only 200 jets
perspectives.summary: China agreed to purchase 200 Boeing jets during Trump-Xi talks, far fewer than analysts expected, sending Boeing shares down 4%. The deal reflects Beijing's cautious approach amid ongoing trade tensions and the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Iraq Seeks IMF Financial Assistance Amid Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: Iraqi officials have approached the IMF about securing financial assistance as a result of the Middle East conflict. The war has disrupted trade routes and energy supplies, straining Iraq's economy.

perspectives.angle: Iraq seeks IMF and World Bank financial assistance amid Iran war fallout
perspectives.summary: Iraqi officials have approached the International Monetary Fund about securing financial assistance as a result of the Middle East conflict, according to a source close to the IMF.

headlines.headline: IMF Welcomes US-China Dialogue as Positive for World Economy
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund welcomed constructive dialogue between Presidents Trump and Xi, saying reduced tensions between the two largest economies benefits global economic stability.

headlines.headline: Global Bonds Tumble as Inflation Fears Mount from Iran War Fallout
headlines.summary: Global bond markets ended a bruising week as economic damage from the Iran conflict prompts investors to expect faster interest rate rises and weaker growth.

perspectives.angle: Russia ramps up energy exports to cushion global markets from Iran war fallout
perspectives.summary: Despite sanctions, Russia is playing a key role cushioning global commodity markets from the Iran war impact, increasing energy exports to stabilize markets amid Middle East supply disruptions.

headlines.headline: IMF Staff to Visit Ukraine for $8.1 Billion Loan Review
headlines.summary: IMF staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to review progress on economic reforms and tax base expansion under an $8.1 billion loan program.

perspectives.angle: IMF Team to Review Ukraine's $8.1 Billion Loan Program
perspectives.summary: International Monetary Fund staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to assess reform progress and tax base expansion under the wartime loan program.

headlines.headline: Turkey and Kazakhstan Deepen Middle Corridor Cooperation
headlines.summary: President Erdogan oversaw the signing of new agreements during a visit to Kazakhstan, expanding the Middle Corridor trade route that bypasses Russian territory. The move strengthens Turkey's role as a logistics hub connecting Asia and Europe.

headlines.headline: Asian Markets Uneven as Trump-Xi Summit Overshadowed by Hormuz Crisis
headlines.summary: Stock markets across Asia showed mixed performance as investors assessed the outcome of the Trump-Xi summit. The closure of the Strait of Hormuz due to the Iran conflict has sent global energy prices soaring, weighing on market sentiment.

headlines.headline: John Bolton: Iran is Europe's War Too
headlines.summary: Former US National Security Adviser John Bolton criticized European nations like Spain for staying out of the Iran conflict, arguing that the war directly affects European security and energy interests.

headlines.headline: Korean Peninsula Sidelined at US-China Summit
headlines.summary: The Korean Peninsula was conspicuously absent from the Trump-Xi summit agenda, signaling Pyongyang's diminishing priority as the US focuses on Iran and trade disputes. Analysts note the shift reflects changing global priorities.

headlines.headline: Analysis: Iran's Future and Its Impact on the South Caucasus
headlines.summary: A new analysis examines three scenarios for Iran's future and how each would affect the strategic landscape of the South Caucasus. Iran borders both Central Asia and the Caucasus, making its stability crucial for regional powers including Armenia, Azerbaijan, and Russia.

headlines.headline: Finland Reopens Helsinki Airport After Drone Alert
headlines.summary: Finnish authorities cleared a drone threat in the capital region, ending a temporary shutdown of Helsinki Airport. Fighter jets were scrambled before the alert was lifted.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia and UAE Conducted Secret Air Strikes on Iran
headlines.summary: US officials revealed that Gulf Arab states carried out covert attacks inside Iran during the US-Israel war, seeking to deter Iran after American security guarantees proved limited.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia and UAE Carried Out Secret Attacks Inside Iran
perspectives.summary: US officials revealed that Gulf Arab states conducted covert operations in Iran, seeking to deter Iranian aggression after the war exposed limits of American security guarantees. The attacks reflect regional unease about Iranian military capabilities.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo Condemns AI Warfare as Path to "Spiral of Annihilation"
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV denounced investments in AI-driven weaponry, warning they lead humanity toward destruction and criticized Europe's military buildup as a betrayal of diplomacy.

headlines.headline: US Abruptly Cancels 4,000-Troop Deployment to Poland
headlines.summary: The Pentagon canceled plans to send 4,000 troops to Poland, raising fresh concerns about US commitment to NATO amid broader discussions about reducing American forces in Europe.

headlines.headline: AP: Trump Insists US-China Relations Good Despite Differences
headlines.summary: As Trump wrapped up his Beijing trip, he maintained that US-China relations are in a good place despite unresolved differences on Taiwan and Iran. The trip focused more on pageantry and symbolism than major breakthroughs.

headlines.headline: CBS: Iran Ready to Repel New US Attack as Peace Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Iran's military said it is trained and ready for any new US assault as peace talks remain stalled. Trump is in China discussing the Iran conflict with Xi Jinping.

perspectives.angle: Iran Says Ready to Repel New US Attack as Peace Talks Stall
perspectives.summary: Iran's military said it is trained and ready for any new US assault as President Trump predicted a "long talk" about the war during his China visit. Peace talks remain stalled with no breakthrough in sight.

headlines.headline: India Hikes Fuel Prices for First Time in Four Years
headlines.summary: India's state-run refiners raised fuel prices for the first time in four years as crude prices surged due to the Iran war, adding pressure to millions facing cooking gas shortages.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia Floats Non-Aggression Pact With Iran
headlines.summary: Riyadh has proposed a regional non-aggression agreement modeled on the 1975 Helsinki Accords, gaining European support but facing uncertainty over US and Israeli backing amid the ongoing Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Riyadh Proposes Non-Aggression Pact Between Iran and Gulf States
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia has proposed a regional security agreement modeled on the 1975 Helsinki Accords, aiming to create a non-aggression pact between Iran and Middle Eastern states amid the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: CENTCOM Chief: Iran Threat Diminished But Not Eliminated
headlines.summary: Admiral Brad Cooper told the Senate Armed Services Committee that the US bombing campaign has degraded 90% of Iran naval mines, but Tehran retains limited strike capability on regional neighbors amid ongoing Strait of Hormuz standoff.

headlines.headline: NPT Review Conference Faces Unprecedented Strain
headlines.summary: The Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty is under stress from five major challenges including US-Israeli strikes on Iran, great power competition, and the Ukraine war, as diplomats meet in New York with modest progress unlikely.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Defense Expert Urges Civilian Drone Production
headlines.summary: A defense researcher called for opening military drone specifications to private manufacturers to reduce costs and enable mass production, after the legislature removed drone procurement from a special defense budget.

headlines.headline: China wants Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions, USTR Greer confirms
headlines.summary: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg that China wants the Strait of Hormuz reopened without curbs or tolls, and that Washington is confident Beijing will limit material support for Iran amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Macron acknowledges France outpaced in Africa by China, Turkey and US
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron acknowledged that France has lost competitive ground in Africa to China, Turkey and the United States, as European influence on the continent continues to decline.

headlines.headline: Solomon Islands elects former China critic Matthew Wale as prime minister
headlines.summary: Parliament elected opposition leader Matthew Wale as prime minister after incumbent Jeremiah Manele was ousted in a no-confidence vote, potentially shifting the Pacific nation's stance on China relations.

perspectives.angle: Solomon Islands Elects China-Cautious Opposition Leader Matthew Wale as PM
perspectives.summary: The Solomon Islands elected Matthew Wale as its new prime minister. Wale has been a critic of the security deal signed with China and has called for it to be made public.

headlines.headline: China wants Strait of Hormuz open without restrictions, USTR Greer says
headlines.summary: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg News that China wants the Strait of Hormuz reopened without curbs or tolls, and expressed confidence Beijing would limit material support for Iran.

perspectives.angle: Xi and Trump Agree Strait of Hormuz Must Remain Open
perspectives.summary: As Trump and Xi held their Beijing summit, both leaders agreed that the Strait of Hormuz must remain open amid the Iran conflict, while a ship was reported seized near the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: US Justice Department set to drop criminal fraud case against India's Gautam Adani
headlines.summary: The U.S. Justice Department is close to dropping criminal fraud charges against Indian billionaire Gautam Adani, who has promised to invest $10 billion in the U.S. economy, according to sources familiar with the matter.

headlines.headline: IMF welcomes constructive US-China dialogue as tensions ease
headlines.summary: The International Monetary Fund said reduced tension and uncertainty between the world's two largest economies would benefit the global economy, as Trump and Xi held their first summit meeting.

perspectives.angle: IMF Welcomes US-China Dialogue as 'Good for World Economy'
perspectives.summary: The International Monetary Fund welcomed the initial positive dialogue between Presidents Trump and Xi Jinping, saying that reduced tensions between the world's two largest economies would benefit the global economy amid ongoing trade and geopolitical uncertainties.

headlines.headline: Latvian Prime Minister Resigns Over Ukrainian Drone Incident Handling
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned, triggering coalition government collapse months before October elections, amid controversy over handling of Ukrainian drone incidents.

perspectives.angle: Latvian PM Resigns Over Handling of Ukrainian Drone Incidents
perspectives.summary: Latvia Prime Minister Evika Silina resigned after coalition ally withdrawal over the government response to Ukrainian drone incursions into Latvian airspace during the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

headlines.headline: IMF Team to Visit Ukraine for $8.1 Billion Loan Program Review
headlines.summary: International Monetary Fund staff will visit Ukraine in coming weeks to review progress on economic reforms and tax base expansion under the major loan program.

headlines.headline: Gazprom Astrakhan Gas Plant Halts Output After Drone Attack
headlines.summary: Russia's Gazprom gas processing plant in Astrakhan halted motor fuel production following a May 13 drone attack that caused a fire at the facility.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Thanks US for Support Ahead of Further Trump-Xi Talks
headlines.summary: Taiwan expressed gratitude to the US for reaffirming support and commitment to peace and stability, as Trump's China visit continues with Taiwan a key topic.

headlines.headline: Israel Pushing for Return to Iran War, Weighing Options With US
headlines.summary: Israeli military officials discussed renewed operations, maritime blockade and other scenarios for resuming conflict with Iran, according to an Israeli broadcaster report.

headlines.headline: Trump Blames Biden for US Decline After Xi Comments on Thucydides Trap
headlines.summary: President Trump invoked his predecessor to explain Chinese President Xi Jinping comments suggesting the US is a declining nation, as the two leaders held high-stakes talks in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Morocco adds B to budget to cushion Middle East conflict impact
headlines.summary: The Moroccan government plans to add 20 billion dirhams to its 2026 budget to finance measures cushioning the impact of the Middle East conflict on the domestic market.

headlines.headline: US cancels planned deployment of 4,000 troops to Poland
headlines.summary: Pentagon scraps temporary troop deployment to Poland, surprising lawmakers and raising questions about US commitment to European defense amid Iran war tensions.

headlines.headline: UK Labour MPs question whether party will survive as political force
headlines.summary: Labour Party faces existential crisis with support at historic lows as internal revolt engulfs Keir Starmer's leadership, raising questions about Britain's political realignment.

headlines.headline: Football unrest triggers protests in Libyan capital
headlines.summary: Protests erupted in Tripoli after football match tensions, with demonstrators damaging parts of the prime minister's office building.

headlines.headline: US Border Patrol chief Michael Banks resigns
headlines.summary: The head of US Border Patrol steps down amid ongoing border security challenges and policy debates.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia condemns Israel over Al-Aqsa Mosque practices
headlines.summary: Saudi foreign ministry denounces Israeli actions at Jerusalem's Al-Aqsa Mosque, calling them provocative amid ongoing regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Macron Faces Backlash in Kenya Over Africa Investment Push
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced a €23 billion investment package for Africa at the Kenya summit, but faced protests and criticism over Frances continued influence in former colonies.

headlines.headline: Hungary summons Russian ambassador over drone attack that entered its airspace
headlines.summary: Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador after a massive drone attack on Ukraine included drones that entered Hungarian airspace, marking a significant diplomatic escalation as Hungary maintains close ties with Moscow.

perspectives.angle: Hungary Summons Russian Ambassador After Zakarpattia Drone Attack
perspectives.summary: Hungary summoned Russias ambassador after Ukraines western Zakarpattia Oblast, home to a Hungarian minority, was targeted in Russias mass drone attack that killed at least six people.

headlines.headline: Iran urges BRICS to condemn US and Israel, Indian-flagged vessel sunk
headlines.summary: Iran's foreign minister called on BRICS nations to condemn violations of international law by the US and Israel, while reports emerged of an Indian-flagged vessel being sunk amid escalating regional tensions.

headlines.headline: India seeks US waiver extension for Russian oil imports amid Iran war
headlines.summary: India has asked the U.S. to extend its waiver allowing purchases of Russian oil, Bloomberg reported, as the ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf disrupts traditional energy supply routes.

perspectives.angle: India Seeks Russian Oil Waiver Extension from US
perspectives.summary: India has asked the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil imports as the Iran war disrupts Persian Gulf energy supplies, Bloomberg News reported.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo criticizes surge in European military spending as diplomacy betrayal
headlines.summary: Pope Leo XIV denounced rising European defense budgets that grew by the highest amount since the Cold War, saying the investment in high-tech weaponry over peace efforts represents a betrayal of diplomacy.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo Condemns European Military Spending Surge as Diplomacy Betrayal
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo criticized the sharp rise in European military spending, the highest since the Cold War, calling it a betrayal of diplomatic solutions amid pressure from the Trump administration.

headlines.headline: Türkiye Backs Iraq New Government Led by PM Al-Zaidi
headlines.summary: Ankara expressed support for Iraq new administration, hoping it will strengthen regional stability and prosperity, reaffirming close cooperation between the two neighbors.

headlines.headline: Ankara Summit to Mark New Chapter for Turkish-American NATO Alliance
headlines.summary: Experts say the upcoming NATO Summit in Ankara will define a new era of Turkish-American cooperation within the alliance, with both countries playing leading roles in NATO transformation.

headlines.headline: Russia Prison Population Falls as Convicts Sent to War
headlines.summary: Ukraine Kyiv Independent reports that Russia prison population has declined significantly as convicts are recruited and sent to fight in Ukraine, revealing another dimension of Russian military manpower strategies.

headlines.headline: Jerusalem Day March Features Death to Arabs Chants and Temple Mount Flag
headlines.summary: Times of Israel reports extremist youth mobs chanted death to Arabs during Jerusalem Day celebrations, while National Security Minister Ben Gvir raised an Israeli flag on the Temple Mount, escalating tensions.

headlines.headline: Nasdaq and S&P 500 hit record highs as geopolitical tensions persist
headlines.summary: US stock markets continued their rally with the Nasdaq and S&P 500 reaching new record highs, defying Iran war tensions and Gulf shipping disruptions. Tech stocks led the gains despite global uncertainty.

headlines.headline: Gold falls as oil prices and dollar rise ahead of Trump-Xi summit
headlines.summary: Gold prices declined as rising oil prices and a stronger dollar pressured markets, with investors awaiting signals from the Trump-Xi meeting. The vessel seizure in the Gulf added to market uncertainty.

perspectives.angle: Global stocks rise on tech rally, oil eases amid Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Strong demand for technology stocks lifted equity indexes worldwide, while oil prices edged down as ships moved through the Strait of Hormuz and investors awaited the outcome of the US-China summit.

perspectives.angle: Chinese yuan hits 3-year high ahead of Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: China's yuan edged up to a three-year high against the dollar, while stock indexes dipped, as investors awaited the outcome of the summit between Trump and Xi.

headlines.headline: IAEA reports over 160 drones near Ukrainian nuclear sites amid safety concerns
headlines.summary: The International Atomic Energy Agency documented a surge in drone activity near Khmelnytskyi, Rivne, and South Ukraine nuclear power plants, raising nuclear safety risks as Zaporizhzhia plant operates on a single backup power line for over seven weeks.

headlines.headline: Nvidia CEO hopes Trump-Xi meeting will improve US-China ties
headlines.summary: Jensen Huang told Chinese state broadcaster CCTV he hopes the Beijing summit will build on the good relationship between the two leaders to improve bilateral relations.

perspectives.angle: Anthropic calls for tighter AI chip export controls as Nvidia CEO joins Trump in Beijing
perspectives.summary: AI company Anthropic released a paper urging the US and allies to maintain chip export restrictions to prevent authoritarian governments from gaining AI leadership, timing the release during US-China summit talks.

headlines.headline: China expresses interest in buying more US oil after Trump-Xi talks
headlines.summary: White House officials raised the prospect of increased Chinese purchases of American energy following the summit between Presidents Trump and Xi in Beijing.

headlines.headline: UK Health Minister Resigns, Calls for Leadership Challenge Against PM Starmer
headlines.summary: Wes Streeting resigned as health minister and called for a leadership contest to oust Prime Minister Keir Starmer, accusing him of political drift. The move opens open rebellion within Labour as others position themselves to challenge the prime minister.

perspectives.angle: UK Ex-Health Minister Streeting to Run Against Starmer for PM
